_comment: Benchmark intake facts for the E-commerce Online Store planning set. Mirrors transactions/answers.json. Planning only — no build. Two answers are marked NEEDS_CONFIRMATION.

intake.pitch: An AdventureWorks Online Store. Customers browse a product catalogue, build a shopping cart, and check out to place orders; Admins manage the product catalogue and stock, process orders through their lifecycle, and manage customer records. Backed by the AdventureWorks 2019 SQL Server database.

signIn.rationale: A real auth API (POST /v1/auth/login, port 10020) issues a cookie-based JWT session validated server-side. RBAC (Customer vs Admin) is enforced by both the front-end and the backend; BFF fits the cookie-session model.

dataSource.note: Microsoft AdventureWorks 2019 sample database on SQL Server. Reuses Production/Sales/Person/Purchasing schemas; adds an AppSecurity schema for identity/RBAC. OpenAPI specs provided (frontend/docs/auth-api.yaml, store-api.yaml). Build against mock data from the specs unless the backend is up.

compliance.note: NEEDS_CONFIRMATION — no regulated/PII compliance regime called out; payment capture is out of scope (order intent only).

styling.note: Use the provided generic-retail design system for colours/fonts/spacing. Sample product fixtures in frontend/docs/products_2026-07-22.csv.

intakeApproval.note: Approve the project facts + page plan once shown.

storyApproval.note: Approve the story list once shown.

perPartChecks: Customer can add a product to the cart and place an order; stock decrements and the cart clears
Admin can advance an order's status (In process -> Approved) and it reflects in the list
Customer session is forbidden (403) from admin product/order/customer endpoints
handsOnApproval.note: Default trio (editable). User-observable checks ticked at MANUAL-TEST.

mergeApproval.note: Approve the merge into main once EPIC-END is green.
